name: GitHub
description: GitHub REST API — issues, PRs, repos, users, actions, and more. The API every developer integrates with but dreads the docs for.

name: Stripe
description: Stripe API — payments, subscriptions, invoices, customers. The gold standard for API design that everyone still struggles to integrate.

name: Slack
description: Slack Web API — messaging, channels, users, bots. The API that powers every team's communication but has the most confusing auth model.

name: Discord
description: Discord API — guilds, channels, messages, webhooks. The API every bot developer needs but the docs are scattered across multiple sites.

name: OpenAI
description: OpenAI API — chat completions, embeddings, models, fine-tuning. The API that launched a thousand startups but changes every 3 months.

name: Notion
description: Notion API — pages, databases, blocks, users. The API for the knowledge base everyone uses but whose data model requires a PhD to understand.

name: Linear
description: Linear API — issues, projects, teams, cycles. The project management API developers actually like, but GraphQL-only means no OpenAPI spec. Pain point: requires custom connector.

name: Jira
description: Jira Cloud REST API — issues, projects, workflows, sprints. The enterprise standard that every developer has been forced to integrate with at least once.

name: Twilio
description: Twilio API — SMS, voice, video, chat. The communications API that powers every verification code you've ever received.

name: Cloudflare
description: Cloudflare API — DNS, workers, zones, firewall rules. The edge platform API that everyone relies on but whose docs are a firehose.
